# DS2API_HISTORY.txt
Prior conversation history and tool progress.

=== 1. SYSTEM ===
You are Cline, a highly skilled software engineer with extensive knowledge in many programming languages, frameworks, design patterns, and best practices.

TOOL USE

You have access to a set of tools that are executed upon the user's approval. You can use one tool per message, and will receive the result of that tool use in the user's response. You use tools step-by-step to accomplish a given task, with each tool use informed by the result of the previous tool use.

# Tool Use Formatting

Tool use is formatted using XML-style tags. The tool name is enclosed in opening and closing tags, and each parameter is similarly enclosed within its own set of tags. Here's the structure:

<tool_name>
<parameter1_name>value1</parameter1_name>
<parameter2_name>value2</parameter2_name>
...
</tool_name>

For example:

<read_file>
<path>src/main.js</path>
<task_progress>
Checklist here (optional)
</task_progress>
</read_file>

Always adhere to this format for the tool use to ensure proper parsing and execution.

# Tools

## execute_command
Description: Request to execute a CLI command on the system. Use this when you need to perform system operations or run specific commands to accomplish any step in the user's task. You must tailor your command to the user's system and provide a clear explanation of what the command does. For command chaining, use the appropriate chaining syntax for the user's shell. Prefer to execute complex CLI commands over creating executable scripts, as they are more flexible and easier to run. Commands will be executed in the current working directory: c:\Users\Admin\ducmanhjr\phat_trien_thi_giac_may_tinh Use @workspace:path syntax (e.g., @frontend:src/index.ts) to specify a workspace.
Parameters:
- command: (required) The CLI command to execute. This should be valid for the current operating system. Ensure the command is properly formatted and does not contain any harmful instructions.
- requires_approval: (required) A boolean indicating whether this command requires explicit user approval before execution in case the user has auto-approve mode enabled. Set to 'true' for potentially impactful operations like installing/uninstalling packages, deleting/overwriting files, system configuration changes, network operations, or any commands that could have unintended side effects. Set to 'false' for safe operations like reading files/directories, running development servers, building projects, and other non-destructive operations.
Usage:
<execute_command>
<command>Your command here</command>
<requires_approval>true or false</requires_approval>
</execute_command>

## read_file
Description: Request to read the contents of a file at the specified path. Use this when you need to examine the contents of an existing file you do not know the contents of, for example to analyze code, review text files, or extract information from configuration files. Returned text lines are prefixed with line labels (e.g. `1 |`, `2 |`). These labels are metadata, not part of the file content. For large files, output is automatically limited to 1000 lines. Use start_line and end_line to read specific sections. Automatically extracts raw text from PDF and DOCX files. May not be suitable for other types of binary files, as it returns the raw content as a string. Do NOT use this tool to list the contents of a directory. Only use this tool on files.
Parameters:
- path: (required) The path of the file to read (relative to the current working directory c:\Users\Admin\ducmanhjr\phat_trien_thi_giac_may_tinh) Use @workspace:path syntax (e.g., @frontend:src/index.ts) to specify a workspace.
- start_line: (optional) The 1-based line number to start reading from (inclusive). Defaults to 1.
- end_line: (optional) The 1-based line number to stop reading at (inclusive). Defaults to start_line + 1000. Use with start_line to read specific sections of large files.
- task_progress: (optional) A checklist showing task progress after this tool use is completed. The task_progress parameter must be included as a separate parameter inside of the parent tool call, it must be separate from other parameters such as content, arguments, etc. (See 'UPDATING TASK PROGRESS' section for more details)
Usage:
<read_file>
<path>File path here</path>
<start_line>1</start_line>
<end_line>1000</end_line>
<task_progress>Checklist here (optional)</task_progress>
</read_file>

## write_to_file
Description: Request to write content to a file at the specified path. If the file exists, it will be overwritten with the provided content. If the file doesn't exist, it will be created. This tool will automatically create any directories needed to write the file.
Parameters:
- path: (required) The path of the file to write to (relative to the current working directory c:\Users\Admin\ducmanhjr\phat_trien_thi_giac_may_tinh) Use @workspace:path syntax (e.g., @frontend:src/index.ts) to specify a workspace.
- content: (required) The content to write to the file. ALWAYS provide the COMPLETE intended content of the file, without any truncation or omissions. You MUST include ALL parts of the file, even if they haven't been modified.
- task_progress: (optional) A checklist showing task progress after this tool use is completed. The task_progress parameter must be included as a separate parameter inside of the parent tool call, it must be separate from other parameters such as content, arguments, etc. (See 'UPDATING TASK PROGRESS' section for more details)

## replace_in_file
Description: Request to replace sections of content in an existing file using SEARCH/REPLACE blocks that define exact changes to specific parts of the file. This tool should be used when you need to make targeted changes to specific parts of a file.
Parameters:
- path: (required) The path of the file to modify (relative to the current working directory c:\Users\Admin\ducmanhjr\phat_trien_thi_giac_may_tinh)
- diff: (required) One or more SEARCH/REPLACE blocks following this exact format:
  ```
  ------- SEARCH
  [exact content to find]
  =======
  [new content to replace with]
  +++++++ REPLACE
  ```
  Critical rules:
  1. SEARCH content must match the associated file section to find EXACTLY:
     * Match character-for-character including whitespace, indentation, line endings
     * Include all comments, docstrings, etc.
  2. SEARCH/REPLACE blocks will ONLY replace the first match occurrence.
     * Including multiple unique SEARCH/REPLACE blocks if you need to make multiple changes.
     * Include *just* enough lines in each SEARCH section to uniquely match each set of lines that need to change.
     * When using multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks, list them in the order they appear in the file.
  3. Keep SEARCH/REPLACE blocks concise:
     * Break large SEARCH/REPLACE blocks into a series of smaller blocks that each change a small portion of the file.
     * Include just the changing lines, and a few surrounding lines if needed for uniqueness.
     * Do not include long runs of unchanging lines in SEARCH/REPLACE blocks.
     * Each line must be complete. Never truncate lines mid-way through as this can cause matching failures.
  4. Special operations:
     * To move code: Use two SEARCH/REPLACE blocks (one to delete from original + one to insert at new location)
     * To delete code: Use empty REPLACE section
  5. If your source context came from read_file and includes line labels (for example, "42 | const x = 1"), do NOT include the "42 | " prefix in SEARCH or REPLACE content. Match only the raw file text.
- task_progress: (optional) A checklist showing task progress after this tool use is completed. The task_progress parameter must be included as a separate parameter inside of the parent tool call, it must be separate from other parameters such as content, arguments, etc. (See 'UPDATING TASK PROGRESS' section for more details)
Usage:
<replace_in_file>
<path>File path here</path>
<diff>Search and replace blocks here</diff>
<task_progress>Checklist here (optional)</task_progress>
</replace_in_file>

Parameters:

This tool should ONLY be used when you have already explored the relevant files and are ready to present a concrete plan. DO NOT use this tool to announce what files you're going to read - just read them first. This tool is only available in PLAN MODE. However, if while writing your response you realize you actually need to do more exploration before providing a complete plan, you can add the optional needs_more_exploration parameter to indicate this. This allows you to acknowledge that you should have done more exploration first, and signals that your next message will use exploration tools instead.

## generate_explanation
Description: Opens a multi-file diff view and generates AI-powered inline comments explaining the changes between two git references. Use this tool to help users understand code changes from git commits, pull requests, branches, or any git refs. The tool uses git to retrieve file contents and displays a side-by-side diff view with explanatory comments.
Parameters:
- title: (required) A descriptive title for the diff view (e.g., 'Changes in commit abc123', 'PR #42: Add authentication', 'Changes between main and feature-branch')
- from_ref: (required) The git reference for the 'before' state. Can be a commit hash, branch name, tag, or relative reference like HEAD~1, HEAD^, origin/main, etc.
- to_ref: (optional) The git reference for the 'after' state. Can be a commit hash, branch name, tag, or relative reference. If not provided, compares to the current working directory (including uncommitted changes).
Usage:
<generate_explanation>
<title>Changes in last commit</title>
<from_ref>HEAD~1</from_ref>
<to_ref>HEAD</to_ref>
</generate_explanation>

# Tool Use Guidelines

1. In <thinking> tags, assess what information you already have and what information you need to proceed with the task.
2. Choose the most appropriate tool based on the task and the tool descriptions provided. Assess if you need additional information to proceed, and which of the available tools would be most effective for gathering this information. For example using the list_files tool is more effective than running a command like `ls` in the terminal. It's critical that you think about each available tool and use the one that best fits the current step in the task.
3. If multiple actions are needed, use one tool at a time per message to accomplish the task iteratively, with each tool use being informed by the result of the previous tool use. Do not assume the outcome of any tool use. Each step must be informed by the previous step's result.
4. Formulate your tool use using the XML format specified for each tool.
5. After each tool use, the user will respond with the result of that tool use. This result will provide you with the necessary information to continue your task or make further decisions. This response may include:
  - Information about whether the tool succeeded or failed, along with any reasons for failure.
  - Linter errors that may have arisen due to the changes you made, which you'll need to address.
  - New terminal output in reaction to the changes, which you may need to consider or act upon.
  - Any other relevant feedback or information related to the tool use.
6. ALWAYS wait for user confirmation after each tool use before proceeding. Never assume the success of a tool use without explicit confirmation of the result from the user.

It is crucial to proceed step-by-step, waiting for the user's message after each tool use before moving forward with the task. This approach allows you to:
1. Confirm the success of each step before proceeding.
2. Address any issues or errors that arise immediately.
3. Adapt your approach based on new information or unexpected results.
4. Ensure that each action builds correctly on the previous ones.

By waiting for and carefully considering the user's response after each tool use, you can react accordingly and make informed decisions about how to proceed with the task. This iterative process helps ensure the overall success and accuracy of your work.

UPDATING TASK PROGRESS

You can track and communicate your progress on the overall task using the task_progress parameter supported by every tool call. Using task_progress ensures you remain on task, and stay focused on completing the user's objective. This parameter can be used in any mode, and with any tool call.

- When switching from PLAN MODE to ACT MODE, you must create a comprehensive todo list for the task using the task_progress parameter
- Todo list updates should be done silently using the task_progress parameter - do not announce these updates to the user
- Use standard Markdown checklist format: "- [ ]" for incomplete items and "- [x]" for completed items
- Keep items focused on meaningful progress milestones rather than minor technical details. The checklist should not be so granular that minor implementation details clutter the progress tracking.
- For simple tasks, short checklists with even a single item are acceptable. For complex tasks, avoid making the checklist too long or verbose.
- If you are creating this checklist for the first time, and the tool use completes the first step in the checklist, make sure to mark it as completed in your task_progress parameter.
- Provide the whole checklist of steps you intend to complete in the task, and keep the checkboxes updated as you make progress. It's okay to rewrite this checklist as needed if it becomes invalid due to scope changes or new information.
- If a checklist is being used, be sure to update it any time a step has been completed.
- The system will automatically include todo list context in your prompts when appropriate - these reminders are important.

Example:
<execute_command>
<command>npm install react</command>
<requires_approval>false</requires_approval>
<task_progress>
- [x] Set up project structure
- [x] Install dependencies

</execute_command>

EDITING FILES

You have access to two tools for working with files: **write_to_file** and **replace_in_file**. Understanding their roles and selecting the right one for the job will help ensure efficient and accurate modifications.

# write_to_file

## Purpose

- Create a new file, or overwrite the entire contents of an existing file.

## When to Use

- Initial file creation, such as when scaffolding a new project.  
- Overwriting large boilerplate files where you want to replace the entire content at once.
- When the complexity or number of changes would make replace_in_file unwieldy or error-prone.
- When you need to completely restructure a file's content or change its fundamental organization.

## Important Considerations

- Using write_to_file requires providing the file's complete final content.  
- If you only need to make small changes to an existing file, consider using replace_in_file instead to avoid unnecessarily rewriting the entire file.
- While write_to_file should not be your default choice, don't hesitate to use it when the situation truly calls for it.

# replace_in_file

## Purpose

- Make targeted edits to specific parts of an existing file without overwriting the entire file.

## When to Use

- Small, localized changes like updating a few lines, function implementations, changing variable names, modifying a section of text, etc.
- Targeted improvements where only specific portions of the file's content needs to be altered.
- Especially useful for long files where much of the file will remain unchanged.

## Advantages

- More efficient for minor edits, since you don't need to supply the entire file content.  
- Reduces the chance of errors that can occur when overwriting large files.

# Choosing the Appropriate Tool

- **Default to replace_in_file** for most changes. It's the safer, more precise option that minimizes potential issues.
- **Use write_to_file** when:
  - Creating new files
  - The changes are so extensive that using replace_in_file would be more complex or risky
  - You need to completely reorganize or restructure a file

175 |         self.min_hits      = min_hits
176 |         self.iou_threshold = iou_threshold
177 |         self.trackers      = []
178 |         self.frame_count   = 0
179 | 
180 |     def update(self, dets):
181 |         """
182 |         dets: np.array shape (N,5) [x1,y1,x2,y2,conf] hoac (0,5) neu khong co det.
183 |         Tra ve: np.array shape (M,5) [x1,y1,x2,y2,track_id]
184 |         """
185 |         self.frame_count += 1
186 | 
187 |         # Predict vi tri moi cho tat ca tracker hien co
188 |         predicted = []
189 |         to_del = []
190 |         for t, trk in enumerate(self.trackers):
191 |             pos = trk.predict()
192 |             if np.any(np.isnan(pos)):
193 |                 to_del.append(t)
194 |             else:
195 |                 predicted.append(pos)
196 |         for t in reversed(to_del):
197 |             self.trackers.pop(t)
198 |         predicted = np.array(predicted) if predicted else np.empty((0, 4))
199 | 
200 |         matched_t = set()
201 |         matched_d = set()
202 |         if len(predicted) > 0 and len(dets) > 0:
203 |             iou_mat = _iou_batch(dets[:, :4], predicted)
204 |             cost    = 1. - iou_mat
205 |             pairs   = _linear_assignment(cost)
206 |             for d_idx, t_idx in pairs:
207 |                 if iou_mat[d_idx, t_idx] >= self.iou_threshold:
208 |                     self.trackers[t_idx].update(dets[d_idx, :4])
209 |                     matched_t.add(t_idx)
210 |                     matched_d.add(d_idx)
211 | 
212 |         # Tao tracker moi cho det chua duoc match
213 |         for d_idx, det in enumerate(dets):
214 |             if d_idx not in matched_d:
215 |                 self.trackers.append(_KalmanBoxTracker(det[:4]))
216 | 
217 |         # Thu thap ket qua, xoa tracker qua han
218 |         ret = []
219 |         keep = []
220 |         for trk in self.trackers:
221 |             if trk.time_since_update <= self.max_age:
222 |                 if trk.hit_streak >= self.min_hits or self.frame_count <= self.min_hits:
223 |                     bbox = trk.get_state()
224 |                     ret.append(np.append(bbox, trk.id))
225 |                 keep.append(trk)
226 |         self.trackers = keep
227 |         return np.array(ret) if ret else np.empty((0, 5))

230 | #  ANALYZER
231 | class Analyzer:
232 |     S_UNSEEN   = 0
233 |     S_ENTERING = 1
234 |     S_TRACKING = 2
235 |     S_VIOLATED = 3
236 |     S_GHOST    = 4
237 | 
238 |     def __init__(self, polygon_pts, pa, pb,
239 |                  enter_confirm=ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
240 |                  vio_confirm=VIOLATION_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
241 |                  cooldown=COOLDOWN_FRAMES):
242 | 
243 |         self.poly_np = np.array(
244 |             [(int(p[0]), int(p[1])) for p in polygon_pts], dtype=np.int32
245 |         )
246 |         self.va = np.array([float(pa[0]), float(pa[1])])
247 |         self.vb = np.array([float(pb[0]), float(pb[1])])
248 | 
249 |         self.enter_confirm = enter_confirm
250 |         self.vio_confirm   = vio_confirm
251 |         self.cooldown_max  = cooldown
252 | 
253 |         self.state      = defaultdict(lambda: self.S_UNSEEN)
254 |         self.enter_cnt  = defaultdict(int)
255 |         self.vio_cnt    = defaultdict(int)
256 |         self.cd_cnt     = defaultdict(int)
257 |         self.ghost_cnt  = defaultdict(int)
258 |         self.prev_pt    = {}
259 |         self.last_bbox  = {}
260 | 
261 |     def _in_zone(self, bbox):
262 |         x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
263 |         cx   = (x1 + x2) // 2
264 | 
265 |         bot  = y2
266 |         mid  = (y1 + y2) // 2
267 |         qbot = (mid + y2) // 2
268 | 
269 |         pts1 = [
270 |             (cx,  bot),
271 |             (x1,  bot),
272 |             (x2,  bot),
273 |             ((x1+cx)//2, bot),
274 |             ((cx+x2)//2, bot),
275 |             (cx,  qbot),
276 |             (x1,  qbot),
277 |             (x2,  qbot),
278 |             (cx,  mid),
279 |         ]
280 |         for px, py in pts1:
281 |             if cv2.pointPolygonTest(self.poly_np, (float(px), float(py)), False) >= 0:
282 |                 return True
283 | 
284 |         # Tang 2: dinh polygon nam trong bbox?
285 |         for vx, vy in self.poly_np:
286 |             if x1 <= vx <= x2 and y1 <= vy <= y2:
287 |                 return True
288 | 
289 |         # Tang 3: grid 3x3 nua duoi bbox
290 |         step_x = max((x2 - x1) // 2, 1)
291 |         step_y = max((y2 - mid) // 2, 1)
292 |         for gx in range(x1, x2 + 1, step_x):
293 |             for gy in range(mid, y2 + 1, step_y):
294 |                 if cv2.pointPolygonTest(self.poly_np, (float(gx), float(gy)), False) >= 0:
295 |                     return True
296 |         return False
297 | 
298 |     @staticmethod
299 |     def _cross2d(ox, oy, ux, uy, vx, vy):
300 |         return (ux - ox) * (vy - oy) - (uy - oy) * (vx - ox)
301 | 
302 |     def _seg_intersects(self, p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y):
303 |         """Kiem tra doan p1Ã¢â€ â€™p2 co cat vach vaÃ¢â€ â€™vb khong (Shamos-Hoey)."""
304 |         ax, ay = self.va[0], self.va[1]
305 |         bx, by = self.vb[0], self.vb[1]
306 |         EPS = 1e-9
307 | 
308 |         d1 = self._cross2d(ax, ay, bx, by, p1x, p1y)
309 |         d2 = self._cross2d(ax, ay, bx, by, p2x, p2y)
310 |         d3 = self._cross2d(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, ax, ay)
311 |         d4 = self._cross2d(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, bx, by)
312 | 
313 |         if ((d1 > 0 and d2 < 0) or (d1 < 0 and d2 > 0)) and \
314 |            ((d3 > 0 and d4 < 0) or (d3 < 0 and d4 > 0)):
315 |             return True
316 | 
317 |         def on_seg(ox, oy, ux, uy, vx, vy):
318 |             return (min(ox,ux)-EPS <= vx <= max(ox,ux)+EPS and
319 |                     min(oy,uy)-EPS <= vy <= max(oy,uy)+EPS)
320 | 
321 |         if abs(d1) < EPS and on_seg(ax, ay, bx, by, p1x, p1y): return True
322 |         if abs(d2) < EPS and on_seg(ax, ay, bx, by, p2x, p2y): return True
323 |         if abs(d3) < EPS and on_seg(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, ax, ay): return True
324 |         if abs(d4) < EPS and on_seg(p1x, p1y, p2x, p2y, bx, by): return True
325 |         return False
326 | 
327 |     def _crosses_vline(self, bbox, prev_bbox, cx, cy, prev):
328 |         if prev is None or prev_bbox is None:
329 |             return False
330 | 
331 |         # Tranh false positive khi xe dung yen
332 |         dist = abs(cy - prev[1]) + abs(cx - prev[0])
333 |         if dist < 1.0:
334 |             return False
335 | 
336 |         if self._seg_intersects(prev[0], prev[1], cx, cy):
337 |             return True
338 | 
339 |         x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
340 |         px1, py1, px2, py2 = [int(v) for v in prev_bbox]
341 |         mx_cur  = (x1+x2)/2.;  my_cur  = (y1+y2)/2.
342 |         mx_prev = (px1+px2)/2.; my_prev = (py1+py2)/2.
343 |         if self._seg_intersects(mx_prev, my_prev, mx_cur, my_cur):
344 |             return True
345 | 
346 |         for (cpx, cpy), (ccx, ccy) in [
347 |             ((px1, py1), (x1, y1)),
348 |             ((px2, py1), (x2, y1)),
349 |             ((px1, py2), (x1, y2)),
350 |             ((px2, py2), (x2, y2)),
351 |         ]:
352 |             if self._seg_intersects(float(cpx), float(cpy),
353 |                                     float(ccx), float(ccy)):
354 |                 return True
355 | 
356 |         return False
357 | 
358 |     def update(self, tid, bbox):
359 |         """
360 |         Tra ve ('tracking'|'violation'|'ghost'|None, bbox_to_draw).
361 |         bbox_to_draw co the la bbox hien tai hoac bbox cu (khi ghost).
362 |         """
363 |         tid = int(tid)
364 |         self.last_bbox[tid]  = bbox
365 |         self.ghost_cnt[tid]  = 0
366 | 
367 |         x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
368 |         cx_cur = (x1 + x2) / 2.
369 |         cy_cur = float(y2)
370 |         prev      = self.prev_pt.get(tid)
371 |         prev_bbox = self.last_bbox.get(tid)
372 |         self.prev_pt[tid] = (cx_cur, cy_cur)
373 | 
374 |         if self.cd_cnt[tid] > 0:
375 |             self.cd_cnt[tid] -= 1
376 |             if self.cd_cnt[tid] == 0:
377 |                 self.state[tid]    = self.S_UNSEEN
378 |                 self.enter_cnt[tid] = 0
379 |                 self.vio_cnt[tid]   = 0
380 |             return 'tracking', bbox
381 | 
382 |         inside = self._in_zone(bbox)
383 | 
384 |         # --- UNSEEN ---
385 |         if self.state[tid] == self.S_UNSEEN:
386 |             if inside:
387 |                 self.state[tid]    = self.S_ENTERING
388 |                 self.enter_cnt[tid] = 1
389 |                 if self.enter_confirm <= 1:
390 |                     self.state[tid] = self.S_TRACKING
391 |                 return 'tracking', bbox
392 |             return None, None
393 | 
394 |         # --- ENTERING ---
395 |         if self.state[tid] == self.S_ENTERING:
396 |             if inside:
397 |                 self.enter_cnt[tid] += 1
398 |                 if self.enter_cnt[tid] >= self.enter_confirm:
399 |                     self.state[tid] = self.S_TRACKING
400 |             else:
401 |                 self.enter_cnt[tid] = max(0, self.enter_cnt[tid] - 1)
402 |                 if self.enter_cnt[tid] == 0:
403 |                     self.state[tid] = self.S_UNSEEN
404 |                     return None, None
405 |             return 'tracking', bbox
406 | 
407 |         # --- TRACKING ---
408 |         if self.state[tid] == self.S_TRACKING:
409 |             if self._crosses_vline(bbox, prev_bbox, cx_cur, cy_cur, prev):
410 |                 self.vio_cnt[tid] += 1
411 |                 if self.vio_cnt[tid] >= self.vio_confirm:
412 |                     self.state[tid]   = self.S_VIOLATED
413 |                     self.cd_cnt[tid]  = self.cooldown_max
414 |                     self.vio_cnt[tid] = 0
415 |                     return 'violation', bbox
416 |             return 'tracking', bbox
417 | 
418 |         # --- VIOLATED (trong cooldown, xu ly o tren) ---
419 |         if self.state[tid] == self.S_VIOLATED:
420 |             self.state[tid]     = self.S_UNSEEN
421 |             self.enter_cnt[tid] = 0
422 |             self.vio_cnt[tid]   = 0
423 |             return None, None
424 | 
425 |         return None, None
426 | 
427 |     def ghost_update(self, tid):
428 | 
429 |         tid = int(tid)
430 |         if self.state[tid] not in (self.S_TRACKING, self.S_ENTERING,
431 |                                    self.S_VIOLATED, self.S_GHOST):
432 |             return None, None
433 |         self.ghost_cnt[tid] += 1
434 |         if self.ghost_cnt[tid] > GHOST_FRAMES:
435 |             self._erase(tid)
436 |             return None, None
437 |         self.state[tid] = self.S_GHOST
438 |         return 'ghost', self.last_bbox.get(tid)
439 | 
440 |     def cleanup(self, active_ids):
441 |         """Goi sau moi frame voi tap ID thuc su con song."""
442 |         active = {int(i) for i in active_ids}
443 |         for tid in list(self.state.keys()):
444 |             if tid not in active and self.state[tid] != self.S_GHOST:
445 |                 # Chuyen sang ghost thay vi xoa ngay
446 |                 if self.state[tid] in (self.S_TRACKING, self.S_ENTERING):
447 |                     self.state[tid] = self.S_GHOST
448 |                     self.ghost_cnt[tid] = 0
449 |                 elif self.state[tid] == self.S_UNSEEN:
450 |                     self._erase(tid)
451 | 
452 |         # Gia tang ghost_cnt cho cac ID dang ghost nhung khong con active
453 |         for tid in list(self.state.keys()):
454 |             if tid not in active and self.state[tid] == self.S_GHOST:
455 |                 self.ghost_cnt[tid] += 1
456 |                 if self.ghost_cnt[tid] > GHOST_FRAMES:
457 |                     self._erase(tid)
458 | 
459 |     def _erase(self, tid):
460 |         for d in (self.state, self.enter_cnt, self.vio_cnt,
461 |                   self.cd_cnt, self.ghost_cnt, self.prev_pt, self.last_bbox):
462 |             d.pop(tid, None)
463 | 
464 |     def get_state(self, tid):
465 |         return self.state.get(int(tid), self.S_UNSEEN)
466 |

531 | #  VIDEO READER (threaded)
532 | class VideoReader(threading.Thread):
533 |     def __init__(self, path, buf=16):
534 |         super().__init__(daemon=True)
535 |         # [OPTIMIZED] Use FFMPEG backend directly for better decoding parallelization
536 |         self.cap   = cv2.VideoCapture(path, cv2.CAP_FFMPEG)
537 |         self.q     = queue.Queue(maxsize=buf)
538 |         self._stop = threading.Event()
539 | 
540 |     @property
541 |     def fps(self):    return self.cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FPS) or 25.0
542 |     @property
543 |     def total(self):  return int(self.cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_COUNT))
544 |     @property
545 |     def width(self):  return int(self.cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH))
546 |     @property
547 |     def height(self): return int(self.cap.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_HEIGHT))
548 | 
549 |     def run(self):
550 |         while not self._stop.is_set():
551 |             ret, frame = self.cap.read()
552 |             if not ret:
553 |                 try: self.q.put(None, timeout=1)
554 |                 except queue.Full: pass
555 |                 break
556 |             try:
557 |                 self.q.put(frame, timeout=0.05)
558 |             except queue.Full:
559 |                 # [OPTIMIZED] Prevent thread from burning 100% CPU when queue is full
560 |                 time.sleep(0.01)
561 |         self.cap.release()
562 | 
563 |     def read(self, timeout=5):
564 |         return self.q.get(timeout=timeout)
565 | 
566 |     def stop(self):
567 |         self._stop.set()
568 |         while not self.q.empty():
569 |             try: self.q.get_nowait()
570 |             except queue.Empty: break

573 | #  GUI DRAWER
574 | class Drawer:
575 |     _PC = (220, 80, 255)
576 |     _VC = (0, 60, 230)
577 |     _HR = 10
578 | 
579 |     def __init__(self, frame):
580 |         self.frame = frame.copy()
581 |         self.H, self.W = frame.shape[:2]
582 |         self._reset()
583 | 
584 |     def _reset(self):
585 |         self.poly   = [list(p) for p in ZONE_POLYGON]
586 |         self.pa     = list(VLINE_A)
587 |         self.pb     = list(VLINE_B)
588 |         self.closed = True
589 |         self._drag  = None
590 | 
591 |     @staticmethod
592 |     def _dist(ax, ay, bx, by):
593 |         return ((ax-bx)**2 + (ay-by)**2) ** 0.5
594 | 
595 |     def _hit(self, x, y):
596 |         th = DRAG_THRESH
597 |         best_d, best_h = float('inf'), None
598 |         for key, pt in (('a', self.pa), ('b', self.pb)):
599 |             d = self._dist(x, y, pt[0], pt[1])
600 |             if d < th and d < best_d:
601 |                 best_d, best_h = d, ('vline', key)
602 |         for i, p in enumerate(self.poly):
603 |             d = self._dist(x, y, p[0], p[1])
604 |             if d < th and d < best_d:
605 |                 best_d, best_h = d, ('poly', i)
606 |         return best_h
607 | 
608 |     def _render(self):
609 |         vis = self.frame.copy()
610 |         if len(self.poly) >= 3:
611 |             pts = np.array(self.poly, np.int32)
612 |             ov  = vis.copy()
613 |             cv2.fillPoly(ov, [pts], self._PC)
614 |             cv2.addWeighted(ov, 0.18, vis, 0.82, 0, vis)
615 |             cv2.polylines(vis, [pts], self.closed, self._PC, 2)
616 |         elif len(self.poly) == 2:
617 |             cv2.line(vis, tuple(self.poly[0]), tuple(self.poly[1]), self._PC, 2)
618 |         for i, p in enumerate(self.poly):
619 |             drag  = (self._drag == ('poly', i))
620 |             r     = self._HR + 3 if drag else self._HR
621 |             color = (0, 255, 120) if drag else self._PC
622 |             cv2.circle(vis, tuple(p), r, color, -1)
623 |             cv2.circle(vis, tuple(p), r, (255,255,255), 1)
624 |             cv2.putText(vis, f"P{i+1}", (p[0]+r+3, p[1]+5),
625 |                         cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.45, (255,255,255), 1)
626 |         if self.poly:
627 |             cx = sum(p[0] for p in self.poly) // len(self.poly)
628 |             cy = sum(p[1] for p in self.poly) // len(self.poly)
629 |             cv2.putText(vis, "LAN CAM", (cx-36, cy),
630 |                         cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.65, (255,255,255), 2)
631 |         ax, ay = self.pa; bx, by = self.pb
632 |         cv2.line(vis, (ax,ay), (bx,by), self._VC, 3)
633 |         for key, pt, lbl in (('a',self.pa,'A'), ('b',self.pb,'B')):
634 |             drag  = (self._drag == ('vline', key))
635 |             r     = self._HR + 3 if drag else self._HR
636 |             color = (0, 200, 255) if drag else self._VC
637 |             cv2.circle(vis, tuple(pt), r, color, -1)
638 |             cv2.circle(vis, tuple(pt), r, (255,255,255), 2)
639 |             cv2.putText(vis, lbl, (pt[0]+r+3, pt[1]+5),
640 |                         cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.6, (255,255,255), 2)
641 |         mx = (ax+bx)//2 - 70; my = (ay+by)//2 - 14
642 |         cv2.putText(vis, "VIOLATION LINE", (mx, my),
643 |                     cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.55, (80,160,255), 2)
644 |         guide = [
645 |             "Trai-click them diem  |  Trai-drag di chuyen  |  Phai-click xoa diem",
646 |             "C: dong/mo poly   Z: undo   R: reset   S: luu   Q: thoat",
647 |             f"Polygon: {len(self.poly)} diem  |  {'DONG' if self.closed else 'MO'}",
648 |         ]
649 |         for i, g in enumerate(guide):
650 |             cv2.putText(vis, g, (10, 22+i*22),
651 |                         cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.50, (220,220,50), 1)
652 |         return vis

654 |     def _on_mouse(self, event, x, y, flags, _):
655 |         if event == cv2.EVENT_LBUTTONDOWN:
656 |             hit = self._hit(x, y)

724 | def nms(dets, iou_thr=0.50):
725 |     """NMS don gian (x1y1x2y2 + conf)."""
726 |     if len(dets) == 0:
727 |         return dets
728 |     x1, y1, x2, y2 = dets[:,0], dets[:,1], dets[:,2], dets[:,3]
729 |     scores = dets[:,4]
730 |     areas  = (x2-x1) * (y2-y1)
731 |     order  = scores.argsort()[::-1]
732 |     keep   = []
733 |     while order.size > 0:
734 |         i = order[0]; keep.append(i)
735 |         if order.size == 1: break
736 |         rest = order[1:]
737 |         xx1 = np.maximum(x1[i], x1[rest])
738 |         yy1 = np.maximum(y1[i], y1[rest])
739 |         xx2 = np.minimum(x2[i], x2[rest])
740 |         yy2 = np.minimum(y2[i], y2[rest])
741 |         inter = np.maximum(0., xx2-xx1) * np.maximum(0., yy2-yy1)
742 |         iou   = inter / (areas[i] + areas[rest] - inter + 1e-9)
743 |         order = rest[iou <= iou_thr]
744 |     return dets[keep]

# ========== UTILITY FUNCTIONS ==========
def point_inside_polygon(pt, poly_np):
    """Check if point (x,y) is inside polygon using ray casting."""
    return cv2.pointPolygonTest(poly_np, (float(pt[0]), float(pt[1])), False) >= 0

def compute_vehicle_speed(prev_pos, cur_pos, time_delta):
    """Compute speed in pixels per second."""
    if prev_pos is None or time_delta <= 0:
        return 0
    dist = np.linalg.norm(np.array(cur_pos) - np.array(prev_pos))
    return dist / time_delta

def adaptive_confirm_frames(speed_px_per_sec, reference_speed=200):
    """Adjust confirm frames based on speed. Faster vehicles need more frames."""
    if not ADAPTIVE_CONFIRM:
        return ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES
    # reference_speed ~ 200 px/sec (typical car crossing)
    ratio = max(0.5, min(2.0, speed_px_per_sec / reference_speed))
    frames = int(ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES * ratio)
    return max(MIN_CONFIRM_FRAMES, min(MAX_CONFIRM_FRAMES, frames))

def adaptive_cooldown_frames(speed_px_per_sec, fps, last_pos, current_pos):
    """Determine cooldown frames based on time and distance."""
    if not ADAPTIVE_COOLDOWN:
        return COOLDOWN_FRAMES
    # Cooldown based on real time
    time_frames = int(COOLDOWN_TIME_SEC * fps)
    # Cooldown based on distance - once vehicle moves far enough, reset
    if last_pos is not None:
        moved = np.linalg.norm(np.array(current_pos) - np.array(last_pos))
        if moved >= COOLDOWN_DISTANCE_PX:
            return 0
    return time_frames

def heading_deviation(velocity_vec, allowed_direction_vec):
    """Compute angle (degrees) between velocity vector and allowed direction."""
    if np.linalg.norm(velocity_vec) < 1e-6:
        return 0
    norm_v = velocity_vec / np.linalg.norm(velocity_vec)
    norm_a = allowed_direction_vec / (np.linalg.norm(allowed_direction_vec) + 1e-6)
    cos_theta = np.clip(np.dot(norm_v, norm_a), -1.0, 1.0)
    return np.arccos(cos_theta) * 180.0 / np.pi

def enhance_low_light(frame):
    """Apply CLAHE to improve night/low-light images."""
    if not LOW_LIGHT_ENHANCE:
        return frame
    lab = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2LAB)
    l, a, b = cv2.split(lab)
    clahe = cv2.createCLAHE(clipLimit=CLAHE_CLIP_LIMIT, tileGridSize=CLAHE_GRID_SIZE)
    l_enh = clahe.apply(l)
    enhanced_lab = cv2.merge((l_enh, a, b))
    enhanced = cv2.cvtColor(enhanced_lab, cv2.COLOR_LAB2BGR)
    return enhanced

# ========== VIDEO RING BUFFER FOR EVIDENCE CLIPS ==========
class VideoRingBuffer:
    """Stores recent frames in memory and can write MP4 clip around violation time."""
    def __init__(self, buffer_seconds, fps, width, height, output_dir):
        self.buffer_seconds = buffer_seconds
        self.fps = fps
        self.width = width
        self.height = height
        self.output_dir = output_dir
        self.buffer_size = int(buffer_seconds * fps)
        self.frames = deque(maxlen=self.buffer_size)
        self.timestamps = deque(maxlen=self.buffer_size)

    def add_frame(self, frame, timestamp_sec):
        self.frames.append(frame.copy())
        self.timestamps.append(timestamp_sec)

    def save_clip(self, violation_time_sec, track_id, output_filename):
        """Save a clip centered around violation_time_sec, length = buffer_seconds."""
        # Find start index: first frame with timestamp >= violation_time_sec - buffer_seconds/2
        half = self.buffer_seconds / 2.0
        start_time = violation_time_sec - half
        end_time = violation_time_sec + half
        clip_frames = []
        for t, f in zip(self.timestamps, self.frames):
            if start_time <= t <= end_time:
                clip_frames.append(f)
        if not clip_frames:
            return
        out_path = os.path.join(self.output_dir, output_filename)
        fourcc = cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*'mp4v')
        writer = cv2.VideoWriter(out_path, fourcc, self.fps, (self.width, self.height))
        for frm in clip_frames:
            writer.write(frm)
        writer.release()
        logger.info(f"Saved video clip: {out_path}")

# ========== LICENSE PLATE RECOGNITION ==========
class PlateRecognizer:
    def __init__(self):
        if not ENABLE_PLATE_RECOGNITION:
            self.reader = None
            return
        # Use EasyOCR with Vietnamese language support
        try:
            self.reader = easyocr.Reader(['en'], gpu=False)  # 'vi' not directly supported, but 'en' works
            logger.info("EasyOCR initialized for license plate recognition")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to initialize EasyOCR: {e}")
            self.reader = None

    def recognize(self, crop_img):
        """Recognize text from cropped license plate image."""
        if self.reader is None or crop_img is None or crop_img.size == 0:
            return ""
        try:
            # Convert BGR to RGB
            rgb = cv2.cvtColor(crop_img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
            results = self.reader.readtext(rgb, paragraph=False)
            if not results:
                return ""
            # Filter by confidence
            texts = [res[1] for res in results if res[2] >= PLATE_CONFIDENCE_THRESH]
            if texts:
                # Join and filter alphanumeric
                plate = ''.join(filter(str.isalnum, texts[0].upper()))
                return plate
        except Exception as e:
            logger.debug(f"Plate recognition error: {e}")
        return ""

# ========== ENHANCED ANALYZER WITH HEADING AND ADAPTIVE PARAMS ==========
class EnhancedAnalyzer:
    """State machine with heading check and dynamic thresholds."""
    S_UNSEEN   = 0
    S_ENTERING = 1
    S_TRACKING = 2
    S_VIOLATED = 3
    S_GHOST    = 4

    def __init__(self, polygon_pts, pa, pb, fps,
                 enter_confirm=ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                 vio_confirm=VIOLATION_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                 cooldown=COOLDOWN_FRAMES):
        self.poly_np = np.array([(int(p[0]), int(p[1])) for p in polygon_pts], dtype=np.int32)
        self.va = np.array([float(pa[0]), float(pa[1])])
        self.vb = np.array([float(pb[0]), float(pb[1])])
        self.fps = fps

        self.base_enter_confirm = enter_confirm
        self.base_vio_confirm = vio_confirm
        self.base_cooldown = cooldown

        self.state = defaultdict(lambda: self.S_UNSEEN)
        self.enter_cnt = defaultdict(int)
        self.vio_cnt = defaultdict(int)
        self.cd_cnt = defaultdict(int)
        self.ghost_cnt = defaultdict(int)
        self.prev_pt = {}
        self.last_bbox = {}
        self.prev_time = {}
        self.speed_history = defaultdict(lambda: deque(maxlen=5))
        self.violation_timestamp = {}
        self.violation_position = {}

    def _in_zone(self, bbox):
        x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
        cx = (x1 + x2) // 2
        bot = y2
        mid = (y1 + y2) // 2
        qbot = (mid + y2) // 2

        pts = [(cx, bot), (x1, bot), (x2, bot), ((x1+cx)//2, bot), ((cx+x2)//2, bot),
               (cx, qbot), (x1, qbot), (x2, qbot), (cx, mid)]
        for (px, py) in pts:
            if point_inside_polygon((px, py), self.poly_np):
                return True
        for vx, vy in self.poly_np:
            if x1 <= vx <= x2 and y1 <= vy <= y2:
                return True
        step_x = max((x2 - x1) // 2, 1)
        step_y = max((y2 - mid) // 2, 1)
        for gx in range(x1, x2+1, step_x):
            for gy in range(mid, y2+1, step_y):
                if point_inside_polygon((gx, gy), self.poly_np):
                    return True
        return False

    def _crosses_vline(self, bbox, prev_bbox):
        if prev_bbox is None:
            return False
        x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
        px1, py1, px2, py2 = [int(v) for v in prev_bbox]
        # Use center line crossing
        def seg_intersect(p1, p2, q1, q2):
            def cross(o, a, b):
                return (a[0]-o[0])*(b[1]-o[1]) - (a[1]-o[1])*(b[0]-o[0])
            o1 = cross(p1, p2, q1)
            o2 = cross(p1, p2, q2)
            o3 = cross(q1, q2, p1)
            o4 = cross(q1, q2, p2)
            if o1 == 0 and min(p1[0], p2[0]) <= q1[0] <= max(p1[0], p2[0]) and min(p1[1], p2[1]) <= q1[1] <= max(p1[1], p2[1]):
                return True
            if o2 == 0 and min(p1[0], p2[0]) <= q2[0] <= max(p1[0], p2[0]) and min(p1[1], p2[1]) <= q2[1] <= max(p1[1], p2[1]):
                return True
            if o3 == 0 and min(q1[0], q2[0]) <= p1[0] <= max(q1[0], q2[0]) and min(q1[1], q2[1]) <= p1[1] <= max(q1[1], q2[1]):
                return True
            if o4 == 0 and min(q1[0], q2[0]) <= p2[0] <= max(q1[0], q2[0]) and min(q1[1], q2[1]) <= p2[1] <= max(q1[1], q2[1]):
                return True
            return (o1 > 0) != (o2 > 0) and (o3 > 0) != (o4 > 0)
        cur_center = ((x1+x2)/2, (y1+y2)/2)
        prev_center = ((px1+px2)/2, (py1+py2)/2)
        return seg_intersect(cur_center, prev_center, self.va, self.vb)

    def _heading_allowed(self, tid, cur_center):
        """Check if vehicle direction is consistent with lane direction."""
        if not HEADING_CHECK:
            return True
        if tid not in self.prev_pt or len(self.prev_pt[tid]) < 2:
            return True
        history = list(self.prev_pt[tid])[-5:]  # last 5 positions
        if len(history) < 2:
            return True
        # Compute average velocity
        vel = np.array([0.0, 0.0])
        for i in range(1, len(history)):
            vel += np.array(history[i]) - np.array(history[i-1])
        vel = vel / (len(history)-1)
        # Allowed direction: from VLINE_A to VLINE_B (or opposite depending on traffic flow)
        allowed = np.array(self.vb) - np.array(self.va)
        dev = heading_deviation(vel, allowed)
        return dev <= HEADING_THRESHOLD_DEG

    def update(self, tid, bbox, frame_time=None):
        tid = int(tid)
        self.last_bbox[tid] = bbox
        self.ghost_cnt[tid] = 0

        x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
        cx_cur = (x1 + x2) / 2.0
        cy_cur = y2
        prev_pos = self.prev_pt.get(tid)
        self.prev_pt[tid] = (cx_cur, cy_cur)

        # Compute speed (px/sec)
        speed = 0
        if prev_pos is not None and frame_time is not None and tid in self.prev_time:
            dt = frame_time - self.prev_time[tid]
            if dt > 0:
                dist = np.linalg.norm(np.array([cx_cur, cy_cur]) - np.array(prev_pos))
                speed = dist / dt
        self.speed_history[tid].append(speed)
        avg_speed = np.mean(list(self.speed_history[tid])) if self.speed_history[tid] else 0
        self.prev_time[tid] = frame_time if frame_time is not None else time.time()

        # Adaptive thresholds
        enter_conf = adaptive_confirm_frames(avg_speed)
        # Check cooldown
        if self.cd_cnt[tid] > 0:
            # Decrement based on real frames
            self.cd_cnt[tid] -= 1
            # Also check distance-based reset for adaptive cooldown
            if ADAPTIVE_COOLDOWN and tid in self.violation_position:
                curr_pos = (cx_cur, cy_cur)
                if np.linalg.norm(np.array(curr_pos) - np.array(self.violation_position[tid])) >= COOLDOWN_DISTANCE_PX:
                    self.cd_cnt[tid] = 0
            if self.cd_cnt[tid] == 0:
                self.state[tid] = self.S_UNSEEN
                self.enter_cnt[tid] = 0
                self.vio_cnt[tid] = 0
            return 'tracking', bbox

        inside = self._in_zone(bbox)

        # UNSEEN
        if self.state[tid] == self.S_UNSEEN:
            if inside:
                self.state[tid] = self.S_ENTERING
                self.enter_cnt[tid] = 1
                if enter_conf <= 1:
                    self.state[tid] = self.S_TRACKING
                return 'tracking', bbox
            return None, None

        # ENTERING
        if self.state[tid] == self.S_ENTERING:
            if inside:
                self.enter_cnt[tid] += 1
                if self.enter_cnt[tid] >= enter_conf:
                    self.state[tid] = self.S_TRACKING
            else:
                self.enter_cnt[tid] = max(0, self.enter_cnt[tid] - 1)
                if self.enter_cnt[tid] == 0:
                    self.state[tid] = self.S_UNSEEN
                    return None, None
            return 'tracking', bbox

        # TRACKING
        if self.state[tid] == self.S_TRACKING:
            crossing = self._crosses_vline(bbox, self.last_bbox.get(tid))
            heading_ok = self._heading_allowed(tid, (cx_cur, cy_cur))
            if crossing and heading_ok:
                self.vio_cnt[tid] += 1
                if self.vio_cnt[tid] >= self.base_vio_confirm:
                    self.state[tid] = self.S_VIOLATED
                    # Adaptive cooldown frames
                    cooldown = adaptive_cooldown_frames(avg_speed, self.fps,
                                                         self.violation_position.get(tid), (cx_cur, cy_cur))
                    self.cd_cnt[tid] = cooldown if cooldown > 0 else self.base_cooldown
                    self.violation_timestamp[tid] = frame_time if frame_time is not None else time.time()
                    self.violation_position[tid] = (cx_cur, cy_cur)
                    self.vio_cnt[tid] = 0
                    return 'violation', bbox
            return 'tracking', bbox

        # VIOLATED (covered by cooldown)
        if self.state[tid] == self.S_VIOLATED:
            self.state[tid] = self.S_UNSEEN
            self.enter_cnt[tid] = 0
            self.vio_cnt[tid] = 0
            return None, None

        return None, None

    def ghost_update(self, tid):
        tid = int(tid)
        if self.state[tid] not in (self.S_TRACKING, self.S_ENTERING, self.S_VIOLATED, self.S_GHOST):
            return None, None
        self.ghost_cnt[tid] += 1
        if self.ghost_cnt[tid] > GHOST_FRAMES:
            self._erase(tid)
            return None, None
        self.state[tid] = self.S_GHOST
        return 'ghost', self.last_bbox.get(tid)

    def cleanup(self, active_ids):
        active = {int(i) for i in active_ids}
        for tid in list(self.state.keys()):
            if tid not in active and self.state[tid] != self.S_GHOST:
                if self.state[tid] in (self.S_TRACKING, self.S_ENTERING):
                    self.state[tid] = self.S_GHOST
                    self.ghost_cnt[tid] = 0
                elif self.state[tid] == self.S_UNSEEN:
                    self._erase(tid)
        for tid in list(self.state.keys()):
            if tid not in active and self.state[tid] == self.S_GHOST:
                self.ghost_cnt[tid] += 1
                if self.ghost_cnt[tid] > GHOST_FRAMES:
                    self._erase(tid)

    def _erase(self, tid):
        for d in (self.state, self.enter_cnt, self.vio_cnt, self.cd_cnt,
                  self.ghost_cnt, self.prev_pt, self.last_bbox, self.prev_time,
                  self.speed_history, self.violation_timestamp, self.violation_position):
            d.pop(tid, None)

    def get_state(self, tid):
        return self.state.get(int(tid), self.S_UNSEEN)

# ========== ENHANCED VIOLATION SAVER WITH VIDEO CLIP & PLATE ==========
class EnhancedViolationSaver:
    def __init__(self, output_dir, ring_buffer=None, plate_recognizer=None):
        self.out = output_dir
        self.count = 0
        os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
        self.ring_buffer = ring_buffer
        self.plate_recognizer = plate_recognizer
        self._log_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "violations.csv")
        with open(self._log_path, 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            csv.writer(f).writerow(['#', 'track_id', 'frame', 'time_s', 'plate', 'base', 'video_clip'])

    def save(self, frame, track_id, bbox, frame_idx, fps, best_info=None, violation_time=None, plate_text=""):
        self.count += 1
        ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S_%f")[:19]
        x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
        fh, fw = frame.shape[:2]
        pw = max(int((x2-x1)*0.3), 30)
        ph = max(int((y2-y1)*0.3), 30)
        crop = frame[max(0,y1-ph):min(fh,y2+ph), max(0,x1-pw):min(fw,x2+pw)]
        t_s = frame_idx / max(fps, 1)
        base = f"vp_{self.count:04d}_id{track_id}_{ts}"

        # Save best frame if provided
        best_crop_path = None
        if best_info and len(best_info) > 0:
            bf, bb, bf_idx, _ = best_info[0]
            bx1, by1, bx2, by2 = [int(v) for v in bb]
            bh, bw = bf.shape[:2]
            bpw = max(int((bx2-bx1)*0.3), 30)
            bph = max(int((by2-by1)*0.3), 30)
            best_crop = bf[max(0,by1-bph):min(bh,by2+bph), max(0,bx1-bpw):min(bw,bx2+bpw)]
            best_crop_path = os.path.join(self.out, base+"_best.jpg")
            cv2.imwrite(best_crop_path, best_crop, [cv2.IMWRITE_JPEG_QUALITY, 95])

        # Save crop
        cv2.imwrite(os.path.join(self.out, base+"_crop.jpg"), crop, [cv2.IMWRITE_JPEG_QUALITY, 92])

        # Save scene
        scene = frame.copy()
        cv2.rectangle(scene, (x1,y1), (x2,y2), (0,0,220), 3)
        label = f"VI PHAM ID:{track_id}"
        if plate_text:
            label += f" | BS:{plate_text}"
        cv2.rectangle(scene, (x1, max(y1-35, 0)), (x1+300, y1), (0,0,0), -1)
        cv2.putText(scene, label, (x1, max(y1-12, 20)), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.85, (0,0,220), 2)
        cv2.putText(scene, f"t={t_s:.1f}s  frame={frame_idx}", (x1, y2+26), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.65, (0,0,220), 2)
        scene_path = os.path.join(self.out, base+"_scene.jpg")
        cv2.imwrite(scene_path, scene, [cv2.IMWRITE_JPEG_QUALITY, 92])

        # Save video clip if ring buffer available
        video_clip_path = ""
        if self.ring_buffer and violation_time is not None and VIDEO_EVIDENCE:
            clip_filename = base + "_evidence.mp4"
            self.ring_buffer.save_clip(violation_time, track_id, clip_filename)
            video_clip_path = clip_filename

        # Save to CSV
        with open(self._log_path, 'a', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            writer = csv.writer(f)
            writer.writerow([self.count, track_id, frame_idx, f"{t_s:.2f}", plate_text, base, video_clip_path])

        logger.info(f"Violation #{self.count} | ID={track_id} | Plate={plate_text} | Frame={frame_idx}")
        return base

# ========== MAIN DETECTION PIPELINE (ENHANCED) ==========
def run_detection_enhanced(show=False):
    if not ULTRALYTICS_AVAILABLE:
        logger.error("Ultralytics YOLO not available.")
        return

    # Initialize reader
    reader = cv2.VideoCapture(VIDEO_PATH)
    if not reader.isOpened():
        logger.error(f"Cannot open video: {VIDEO_PATH}")
        return
    fps = reader.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FPS) or 25.0
    total_frames = int(reader.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_COUNT))
    W = int(reader.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_WIDTH))
    H = int(reader.get(cv2.CAP_PROP_FRAME_HEIGHT))
    reader.release()

    # Setup video reader with threading
    class ThreadedReader:
        def __init__(self, path, queue_size=16):
            self.cap = cv2.VideoCapture(path, cv2.CAP_FFMPEG)
            self.q = queue.Queue(maxsize=queue_size)
            self.stop = threading.Event()
            self.thread = threading.Thread(target=self._fill, daemon=True)
            self.thread.start()
        def _fill(self):
            while not self.stop.is_set():
                ret, frame = self.cap.read()
                if not ret:
                    self.q.put(None)
                    break
                self.q.put(frame)
        def read(self):
            return self.q.get(timeout=5)
        def release(self):
            self.stop.set()
            self.thread.join(timeout=1)
            self.cap.release()
    threaded_reader = ThreadedReader(VIDEO_PATH)

    # Initialize YOLO model
    model = YOLO(YOLO_MODEL)

    # Determine inference size
    infer_h = int(INFER_W * H / W)
    scale_x = W / INFER_W
    scale_y = H / infer_h

    # Prepare tracker arguments
    tracker_args = {
        'tracker': TRACKER_TYPE,
        'conf': TRACKER_CONF,
        'iou': TRACKER_IOU,
        'verbose': False
    }

    # Create ring buffer for video evidence
    ring_buffer = None
    if VIDEO_EVIDENCE:
        ring_buffer = VideoRingBuffer(VIDEO_EVIDENCE_SECONDS, fps, W, H, OUTPUT_DIR)

    # Plate recognizer
    plate_recognizer = PlateRecognizer() if ENABLE_PLATE_RECOGNITION else None

    # Setup analyzer and saver
    run_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    run_dir = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, f"run_{run_ts}")
    os.makedirs(run_dir, exist_ok=True)

    analyzer = EnhancedAnalyzer(ZONE_POLYGON, VLINE_A, VLINE_B, fps,
                                ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES, VIOLATION_CONFIRM_FRAMES, COOLDOWN_FRAMES)
    saver = EnhancedViolationSaver(run_dir, ring_buffer, plate_recognizer)

    # Visualization state
    S = EnhancedAnalyzer
    COLOR = {
        S.S_ENTERING: (0, 200, 200),
        S.S_TRACKING: (0, 200, 50),
        S.S_VIOLATED: (0, 40, 220),
        S.S_GHOST: (150, 150, 0),
    }
    LABEL = {
        S.S_ENTERING: "ENTER",
        S.S_TRACKING: "TRACK",
        S.S_VIOLATED: "VI PHAM!",
        S.S_GHOST: "GHOST",
    }

    # Build overlay for display
    zone_overlay = np.zeros((H, W, 3), dtype=np.uint8)
    pts = np.array([(int(p[0]), int(p[1])) for p in ZONE_POLYGON], np.int32)
    cv2.fillPoly(zone_overlay, [pts], (180, 60, 220))
    cv2.polylines(zone_overlay, [pts], True, (220, 80, 255), 2)
    cv2.line(zone_overlay, VLINE_A, VLINE_B, (0, 50, 220), 3)
    cx = int(np.mean([p[0] for p in ZONE_POLYGON]))
    cy = int(np.mean([p[1] for p in ZONE_POLYGON]))
    cv2.putText(zone_overlay, "LAN CAM DI THANG", (cx-80, cy), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.65, (255,255,255), 2)
    mx = (VLINE_A[0]+VLINE_B[0])//2 - 70
    my = (VLINE_A[1]+VLINE_B[1])//2 - 14
    cv2.putText(zone_overlay, "VIOLATION LINE", (mx, my), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.55, (80,160,255), 2)

    # Main loop
    frame_idx = 0
    spf = 1.0 / max(fps, 1)
    track_cls = {}
    best_frames = {}   # {tid: [(frame, bbox, idx, area)]}
    pending_vio = set()

    logger.info(f"Starting enhanced detection on {VIDEO_PATH} | FPS={fps:.2f} | Res={W}x{H}")
    try:
        while True:
            t0 = time.perf_counter()
            frame = threaded_reader.read()
            if frame is None:
                break

            # Low-light enhancement
            frame_processed = enhance_low_light(frame)

            # Resize for inference
            small = cv2.resize(frame_processed, (INFER_W, infer_h), interpolation=cv2.INTER_LINEAR)

            # Run YOLO tracking
            results = model.track(small, persist=True, **tracker_args,
                                  classes=list(VEHICLE_CLASSES), conf=CONF_THRESH, iou=IOU_THRESH, verbose=False)

            # Extract detections and track IDs
            dets = []   # list of [x1,y1,x2,y2,conf,track_id?]
            track_ids = []
            if results[0].boxes is not None and len(results[0].boxes) > 0:
                boxes = results[0].boxes
                xyxy = boxes.xyxy.cpu().numpy()
                confs = boxes.conf.cpu().numpy()
                clses = boxes.cls.cpu().numpy().astype(int)
                # Get track IDs if available (using .id attribute)
                if boxes.id is not None:
                    ids = boxes.id.cpu().numpy().astype(int)
                else:
                    ids = [-1] * len(xyxy)
                for i in range(len(xyxy)):
                    x1o = xyxy[i][0] * scale_x
                    y1o = xyxy[i][1] * scale_y
                    x2o = xyxy[i][2] * scale_x
                    y2o = xyxy[i][3] * scale_y
                    dets.append([x1o, y1o, x2o, y2o, confs[i]])
                    track_ids.append(ids[i])
                    track_cls[ids[i]] = clses[i] if ids[i] != -1 else -1

            # Convert to numpy for simplicity, but we'll use track_ids for mapping
            active_ids = []
            draw_ids = {}
            current_time = time.time()

            # Group detections by track_id (for those with valid ID)
            # For simplicity, we iterate over all detections and treat each as its own track
            # But YOLO tracking already assigns IDs; we can directly use them.
            for i, det in enumerate(dets):
                tid = track_ids[i]
                if tid == -1:
                    continue   # skip untracked detections (should not happen if tracker is used)
                bbox = np.array(det[:4])
                active_ids.append(tid)
                result, draw_bbox = analyzer.update(tid, bbox, current_time)
                if result in ('tracking', 'violation'):
                    area = (bbox[2]-bbox[0])*(bbox[3]-bbox[1])
                    if tid not in best_frames:
                        best_frames[tid] = []
                    best_frames[tid].append((frame.copy(), bbox.copy(), frame_idx, area))
                    best_frames[tid].sort(key=lambda x: x[3], reverse=True)
                    best_frames[tid] = best_frames[tid][:5]
                if result == 'violation':
                    pending_vio.add(tid)
                    draw_ids[tid] = (draw_bbox, S.S_VIOLATED)
                elif result == 'tracking':
                    draw_ids[tid] = (draw_bbox, analyzer.get_state(tid))

            # Process pending violations (save when ID disappears)
            for tid in list(pending_vio):
                if tid not in active_ids:
                    b_info = best_frames.pop(tid, None)
                    l_bbox = analyzer.last_bbox.get(tid)
                    if l_bbox is not None:
                        # Try to recognize plate from best crop
                        plate_text = ""
                        if plate_recognizer and b_info and len(b_info) > 0:
                            best_crop_frame = b_info[0][0]
                            # Extract crop around vehicle
                            bx1, by1, bx2, by2 = [int(v) for v in b_info[0][1]]
                            fh, fw = best_crop_frame.shape[:2]
                            pw = max(int((bx2-bx1)*0.3), 30)
                            ph = max(int((by2-by1)*0.3), 30)
                            crop_plate = best_crop_frame[max(0,by1-ph):min(fh,by2+ph), max(0,bx1-pw):min(fw,bx2+pw)]
                            if crop_plate.size > 0:
                                plate_text = plate_recognizer.recognize(crop_plate)
                        violation_time = analyzer.violation_timestamp.get(tid)
                        saver.save(frame, tid, l_bbox, frame_idx, fps, b_info, violation_time, plate_text)
                    pending_vio.remove(tid)

            # Cleanup best_frames
            for tid in list(best_frames.keys()):
                if tid not in active_ids and tid not in pending_vio:
                    best_frames.pop(tid, None)

            # Add frame to ring buffer for video evidence
            if ring_buffer:
                ring_buffer.add_frame(frame, frame_idx / fps)

            # Visualization
            if show:
                vis = cv2.addWeighted(frame, 0.85, zone_overlay, 0.15, 0)
                cv2.line(vis, VLINE_A, VLINE_B, (0, 40, 220), 3)
                cv2.circle(vis, VLINE_A, 7, (0, 180, 255), -1)
                cv2.circle(vis, VLINE_B, 7, (0, 40, 220), -1)
                for tid, (bbox, state) in draw_ids.items():
                    if bbox is None:
                        continue
                    x1, y1, x2, y2 = [int(v) for v in bbox]
                    color = COLOR.get(state, (180,180,180))
                    lbl = LABEL.get(state, "")
                    thick = 3 if state == S.S_VIOLATED else 2
                    if state == S.S_GHOST:
                        ghost_vis = vis.copy()
                        cv2.rectangle(ghost_vis, (x1,y1), (x2,y2), color, thick)
                        cv2.addWeighted(ghost_vis, 0.5, vis, 0.5, 0, vis)
                    else:
                        cv2.rectangle(vis, (x1,y1), (x2,y2), color, thick)
                    bc = ((x1+x2)//2, y2)
                    cv2.circle(vis, bc, 5, color, -1)
                    cls_name = VEHICLE_NAMES.get(track_cls.get(tid, -1), "")
                    tag = f"ID:{tid} {cls_name} {lbl}".strip()
                    (tw, th), _ = cv2.getTextSize(tag, cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.52, 2)
                    lx, ly = x1, max(y1-6, 16)
                    cv2.rectangle(vis, (lx, ly-th-4), (lx+tw+6, ly+2), (0,0,0), -1)
                    cv2.putText(vis, tag, (lx+3, ly), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.52, color, 2)

                n_track = sum(1 for _, s in draw_ids.values() if s == S.S_TRACKING)
                hud = f"Frame {frame_idx}/{total_frames} | Trong lan: {n_track} | Vi pham: {saver.count}"
                (hw, hh), _ = cv2.getTextSize(hud, cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.75, 2)
                cv2.rectangle(vis, (0,0), (hw+16, 44), (0,0,0), -1)
                cv2.putText(vis, hud, (8, 30), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.75, (255,230,0), 2)
                cv2.imshow("Enhanced Wrong Lane Detector", vis)
                elapsed = time.perf_counter() - t0
                wait_ms = max(1, int((spf - elapsed) * 1000))
                if cv2.waitKey(wait_ms) & 0xFF == ord('q'):
                    break

            frame_idx += 1
            if frame_idx % 60 == 0:
                logger.info(f"Frame {frame_idx}/{total_frames} | Active tracks: {len(active_ids)} | Violations: {saver.count}")

    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        logger.info("Interrupted by user.")
    finally:
        threaded_reader.release()
        try:
            cv2.destroyAllWindows()
        except:
            pass
        logger.info(f"Total violations: {saver.count}")
        logger.info(f"Evidence saved in: {os.path.abspath(run_dir)}")

# ========== DRAWER (unchanged, but uses new config) ==========
class Drawer:
    _PC = (220, 80, 255)
    _VC = (0, 60, 230)
    _HR = 10
    DRAG_THRESH = 16

    def __init__(self, frame):
        self.frame = frame.copy()
        self.H, self.W = frame.shape[:2]
        self._reset()
        self._drag = None

    def _reset(self):
        self.poly = [list(p) for p in ZONE_POLYGON]
        self.pa = list(VLINE_A)
        self.pb = list(VLINE_B)
        self.closed = True

    def _dist(self, ax, ay, bx, by):
        return ((ax-bx)**2 + (ay-by)**2) ** 0.5

    def _hit(self, x, y):
        th = self.DRAG_THRESH
        best_d, best_h = float('inf'), None
        for key, pt in (('a', self.pa), ('b', self.pb)):
            d = self._dist(x, y, pt[0], pt[1])
            if d < th and d < best_d:
                best_d, best_h = d, ('vline', key)
        for i, p in enumerate(self.poly):
            d = self._dist(x, y, p[0], p[1])
            if d < th and d < best_d:
                best_d, best_h = d, ('poly', i)
        return best_h

    def _render(self):
        vis = self.frame.copy()
        if len(self.poly) >= 3:
            pts = np.array(self.poly, np.int32)
            ov = vis.copy()
            cv2.fillPoly(ov, [pts], self._PC)
            cv2.addWeighted(ov, 0.18, vis, 0.82, 0, vis)
            cv2.polylines(vis, [pts], self.closed, self._PC, 2)
        elif len(self.poly) == 2:
            cv2.line(vis, tuple(self.poly[0]), tuple(self.poly[1]), self._PC, 2)
        for i, p in enumerate(self.poly):
            drag = (self._drag == ('poly', i))
            r = self._HR+3 if drag else self._HR
            color = (0,255,120) if drag else self._PC
            cv2.circle(vis, tuple(p), r, color, -1)
            cv2.circle(vis, tuple(p), r, (255,255,255), 1)
            cv2.putText(vis, f"P{i+1}", (p[0]+r+3, p[1]+5), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.45, (255,255,255), 1)
        if self.poly:
            cx = sum(p[0] for p in self.poly)//len(self.poly)
            cy = sum(p[1] for p in self.poly)//len(self.poly)
            cv2.putText(vis, "LAN CAM", (cx-36, cy), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.65, (255,255,255), 2)
        ax, ay = self.pa; bx, by = self.pb
        cv2.line(vis, (ax,ay), (bx,by), self._VC, 3)
        for key, pt, lbl in (('a',self.pa,'A'), ('b',self.pb,'B')):
            drag = (self._drag == ('vline', key))
            r = self._HR+3 if drag else self._HR
            color = (0,200,255) if drag else self._VC
            cv2.circle(vis, tuple(pt), r, color, -1)
            cv2.circle(vis, tuple(pt), r, (255,255,255), 2)
            cv2.putText(vis, lbl, (pt[0]+r+3, pt[1]+5), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.6, (255,255,255), 2)
        mx = (ax+bx)//2 -70; my = (ay+by)//2 -14
        cv2.putText(vis, "VIOLATION LINE", (mx, my), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.55, (80,160,255), 2)
        guide = [
            "Left-click add point | Left-drag move | Right-click delete",
            "C: close/open poly | Z: undo | R: reset | S: save | Q: quit",
            f"Polygon: {len(self.poly)} points | {'CLOSED' if self.closed else 'OPEN'}",
        ]
        for i, g in enumerate(guide):
            cv2.putText(vis, g, (10, 22+i*22), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.50, (220,220,50), 1)
        return vis

    def _on_mouse(self, event, x, y, flags, _):
        if event == cv2.EVENT_LBUTTONDOWN:
            hit = self._hit(x, y)
            if hit:
                self._drag = hit
            else:
                self.poly.append([x, y])
                self._drag = ('poly', len(self.poly)-1)
        elif event == cv2.EVENT_MOUSEMOVE and (flags & cv2.EVENT_FLAG_LBUTTON):
            if self._drag is None:
                return
            kind, ref = self._drag
            if kind == 'poly':
                self.poly[ref] = [x, y]
            elif ref == 'a':
                self.pa = [x, y]
            else:
                self.pb = [x, y]
        elif event == cv2.EVENT_LBUTTONUP:
            self._drag = None
        elif event == cv2.EVENT_RBUTTONDOWN:
            hit = self._hit(x, y)
            if hit and hit[0] == 'poly' and len(self.poly) > 1:
                self.poly.pop(hit[1])

    def run(self):
        cv2.namedWindow("Drawer - Wrong Lane Config", cv2.WINDOW_NORMAL)
        cv2.setMouseCallback("Drawer - Wrong Lane Config", self._on_mouse)
        while True:
            cv2.imshow("Drawer - Wrong Lane Config", self._render())
            key = cv2.waitKey(20) & 0xFF
            if key == ord('c'):
                self.closed = not self.closed
            elif key == ord('z') and self.poly:
                self.poly.pop()
            elif key == ord('r'):
                self._reset()
            elif key == ord('s'):
                if len(self.poly) < 3:
                    print("[WARN] Need at least 3 polygon points!")
                    continue
                cfg = {
                    "zone_polygon": self.poly,
                    "vline_a": self.pa,
                    "vline_b": self.pb,
                    "enter_confirm_frames": ENTER_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                    "violation_confirm_frames": VIOLATION_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                    "cooldown_frames": COOLDOWN_FRAMES,
                    "adaptive_confirm": ADAPTIVE_CONFIRM,
                    "adaptive_cooldown": ADAPTIVE_COOLDOWN,
                    "min_confirm_frames": MIN_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                    "max_confirm_frames": MAX_CONFIRM_FRAMES,
                    "cooldown_time_sec": COOLDOWN_TIME_SEC,
                    "cooldown_distance_px": COOLDOWN_DISTANCE_PX,
                    "heading_check": HEADING_CHECK,
                    "heading_threshold_deg": HEADING_THRESHOLD_DEG,
                    "low_light_enhance": LOW_LIGHT_ENHANCE,
                    "clahe_clip_limit": CLAHE_CLIP_LIMIT,
                    "clahe_grid_size": list(CLAHE_GRID_SIZE),
                    "video_evidence": VIDEO_EVIDENCE,
                    "video_evidence_seconds": VIDEO_EVIDENCE_SECONDS,
                    "enable_plate_recognition": ENABLE_PLATE_RECOGNITION,
                    "plate_confidence_thresh": PLATE_CONFIDENCE_THRESH,
                    "structured_logging": STRUCTURED_LOGGING,
                    "log_dir": LOG_DIR,
                    "yolo_model": YOLO_MODEL,
                    "tracker_type": TRACKER_TYPE,
                    "tracker_conf": TRACKER_CONF,
                    "tracker_iou": TRACKER_IOU,
                }
                with open(CONFIG_FILE, "w") as f:
                    json.dump(cfg, f, indent=2)
                print(f"[SAVED] {CONFIG_FILE}")
                break
            elif key == ord('q'):
                break
        cv2.destroyAllWindows()
        return self.poly, self.pa, self.pb

# ========== ENTRY POINT ==========
if __name__ == "__main__":
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Enhanced Wrong Lane Detector")
    ap.add_argument("--draw", action="store_true", help="Open GUI to draw zone and violation line")
    ap.add_argument("--run", action="store_true", help="Run detection")
    ap.add_argument("--show", action="store_true", help="Show preview while running")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    if args.draw:
        cap = cv2.VideoCapture(VIDEO_PATH)
        ret, frame = cap.read()
        cap.release()
        if not ret:
            print(f"[ERROR] Cannot read video: {VIDEO_PATH}")
        else:
            d = Drawer(frame)
            poly, pa, pb = d.run()
            if poly:
                print(f"[DRAW] Polygon: {len(poly)} points")
                print(f"[DRAW] Vline: {pa} -> {pb}")
    elif args.run:
        run_detection_enhanced(show=args.show)
    else:
        print(__doc__)
